The Rathe Mountains
This item is meant as a substitute for 100,000 platinum. Unfortunately, the merchants take a 10% "tax" on anyone buying one from them. The item sells back to a merchant for 100,000 platinum.


Uploaded December 4th, 2022 by iventheassassin

Zog Eggrib is "Parcels and Noble Exchange" in the evil camp. Jeim lists the same, but I didn't check him out as I am evil. You may purchase Nobles from Zog for 122,023pp 1g 2s 7c

Quick Question
QuoteReply
# Jun 23 2015 at 8:25 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1670804351699
1 post
Has anyone ever seen the Firestorm cloak for sale at this vendor? I know it's rare and stupid expensive, but I would like to know if it even exists before droping a pile of pp trying for a chance to get it. Judging by the massive amounts of Krono's offered for it I would guess you can't get it any more.

umm- another "plat sink"
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2014 at 8:32 AM Rating: Default
iyeni
View User Forum
Scholar
654 posts
I really don't think we need more "plat sinks". it's not like everyone on this game is a multimillionaire. get real and reasonable please. there is no way i will pay 110k for something worth only 100k. i would appreciate if soe would understand that we don't want to be taxed. tax without representation is tyranny. get rid of it, please. i won't be buying these even though i would like to have what some of the things the merchant will be selling. it simply isn't worth it to me. it's not like there are great stats on the items. we will use them to decorate with mostly.
____________________________
Success comes when preparation meets opportunity -- and EQ is no exception.
▲ Page top
umm- another "plat sink"
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2014 at 9:12 AM Rating: Decent
nekokirei
Guru
362 posts
iyeni wrote:
I really don't think we need more "plat sinks".

Yeah, considering the platflation, not needed at all...
iyeni wrote:
it's not like everyone on this game is a multimillionaire.

I've been back just over a year -- I have a rather obscene amount of plat.
iyeni wrote:
get real and reasonable please. there is no way i will pay 110k for something worth only 100k.

Then, don't.
iyeni wrote:
i would appreciate if soe would understand that we don't want to be taxed. tax without representation is tyranny. get rid of it, please.

Who ties your shoelaces in the mornings? This isn't a 'tax', it's the value of the exchange token to accommodate the large number of players shuffling about large amounts of plat because of the 2million plat threshold. This not the Colonies and you're not Patrick Henry nor are you in Boston dumping tea in a harbour. FFS.
iyeni wrote:
i won't be buying these even though i would like to have what some of the things the merchant will be selling. it simply isn't worth it to me. it's not like there are great stats on the items. we will use them to decorate with mostly.

Do you read the words you write?
____________________________
--
Savage Lady Nekokirei of Drinal

Other Noble Exchange locations.
QuoteReply
# Sep 24 2014 at 4:37 PM Rating: Good
Merf
View User Forum
1,344 posts
Deputy Fozon in Rivervale.
Deputy Heepo in Rivervale.
Guard Kanuf in North Kaladin.
Karoline at the Butcherblock Wayfarer's Camp. N1135, N2462.
Joff at the Everfrost Wayfarer's Camp at P1900, N5021.
Nablea at the Commonland Wayfarer's Camp at N1873, N2463.
Wollems at the North Ro Watfarer's Camp at P7880, N532.
No Noble Exchange NPCs seen at the South Ro, Abysmal Sea, Nedaria's Landing. or Natimbi Wayfarer Camps.
I have not checked the other banks across the lands, but I'm sure there are more.

# Sep 24 2014 at 3:06 PM Rating: Good
Merf
View User Forum
1,344 posts
This item is meant as a substitute for 100,000 platinum, but it costs 110249pp 9gp 9sp 2cp? Is this Obamanomics?
____________________________
Alliance Artisan Demonicspawn Dealerofdeath, Scoundrel of the Oathbreakers
Ogre Shadow Knight
Vertigo
Tunare - The Seventh Hammer

Ascending Angels on Beta
▲ Page top
HUH?
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2014 at 12:34 PM Rating: Good
Drewinette
View User Forum
1,658 posts
From the post Nobles: A New Alternate Currency Type on SoE forums created by Aristo on September 12th, 2014 :

Quote:
Q: Why is there such a mark-up on the merchant price? They sell back to a merchant for 100,000 platinum, but cost 110,245 platinum to buy!

A: This is the minimum price difference allowed by EverQuest's merchant system. We will watch how people use the system as is and investigate options if it isn't useful.
